name: Raw Gluten
recipeInstructions:
1. Pour the water into a large bowl.
2. Add about 3 cups of the flour and mix well.
3. Beat the dough for about 100 strokes, or until it becomes elastic.
4. Gradually add enough of the remaining flour to make a kneadable dough.
5. Turn the dough out onto a generously floured surface and knead in enough flour to make a firm and non-sticky dough.
6. Knead the dough vigorously for 20 minutes. If kneading becomes too difficult, try pounding the dough with a rolling pin or with your fists.
7. Continue kneading and/or pounding the dough until it becomes very smooth and elastic.
8. To ensure the dough is well-kneaded, break off a small piece and wash it. If it is sufficiently kneaded, the starch will wash away, leaving only the elastic gluten.
9. Fill a large bowl with room-temperature water (use the same bowl used to mix the dough).
10. Place the bowl in the sink and submerge the dough in the water.
11. Work the dough by rubbing and squeezing it between your hands, similar to washing clothes.
12. Do not worry if the dough falls apart; it will come back together as it is washed.
13. When the water becomes very cloudy, pour (with the dough) through a colander.
14. If desired, place an empty bowl under the colander to catch the starch water.
15. Repeat the squeezing and rinsing process until the rinse water is nearly clear and the dough resembles a large, elastic wad of chewing gum.
16. You now have raw gluten. Use it to make seitan.
Makes 1 1/2 to 2 cups from the files of DEEANNE
